title: Memory Allocation Strategies for Multiple Stacks in a Single Array
description: A visual comparison of two common methods for implementing multiple stacks using one shared array, showing their memory allocation and potential overflow conditions.
relationships: Fixed Division: Shows two stacks growing from opposite ends towards a fixed middle boundary, indicating a static allocation.
Flexible Division: Illustrates how stacks can grow dynamically towards each other, sharing free space until the combined size exceeds the total array capacity, leading to overflow.
